extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won a 183km stage to Privas in a bunch sprint after INEOS and JUMBO-VISMA imposed a ferocious pace in crosswinds late in the stage to splinter the field.
tactical_patterns: INEOS and JUMBO-VISMA orchestrated brutal crosswind attacks inside final 20km specifically to fracture sprinter positioning, not GC.
VAN AERT positioned himself in leadout trains rather than relying on dedicated leadout, exploiting sprinter team chaos from earlier splits.
Intermediate sprint saw sprinter teams fully committed early, suggesting they anticipated crosswind aggression would deplete leadout resources later.
SCHÄR Michael launched decoy attack inside 2km to isolate TRENTIN Matteo as sole CCC threat, forcing rivals to chase.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Worked for team in crosswinds then still won sprint, proving diesel engine sprint power after high tempo
rider_signals.BOL Cees: positive: SUNWEB leadout delivered him to perfect third-wheel position inside 1km, lost only to superior finishing speed
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Third place from chaos positioning suggests superior opportunistic sprint instincts when traditional trains collapse
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Started sprint too late or lacked power against VAN AERT, troubling sign given intermediate sprint deficit already
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Eased up before line despite being well-positioned left barriers inside final km, suggests crosswind effort damaged him
rider_signals.MEZGEC Luka: neutral: MITCHELTON-SCOTT invested leadout effort but sprinter finished sixth, indicating team miscalculation or rider form deficit
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: negative: Despite SCHÄR decoy attack for position, could only scramble on inside barriers without organized delivery
